title: kpod built on fedora not usable in minikube
body:
I am Building kpod in fedora 27

```bash
make install.tools 
make
sudo make install
```

kpod version:

```console
$ kpod version
Version:       0.1
Go Version:    go1.9.2
Git Commit:    "195d48d86d871f531d72e0669ea96d315845da35"
Built:         Fri Nov 24 07:52:30 2017
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

Now copying this built kpod to minikube machine to use there:

```conosle
scp `which kpod` docker@192.168.122.117:/home/docker/ 
```

Inside minikube vm

```console
$ ./kpod -h
./kpod: error while loading shared libraries: libostree-1.so.1: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
```

I could not find much help of what should I be doing to get this binary workng in minikube vm.

Minikube VM OS info:

```console
$ cat /etc/os-release 
NAME=Buildroot
VERSION=2017.02
ID=buildroot
VERSION_ID=2017.02
PRETTY_NAME="Buildroot 2017.02"
```

comments.body: ping @TomSweeneyRedHat did you get a chance to try this one?
You need to install libostree into the container as well. Or build kpod without ostree support.
@rhatdan is it possible to do the build without ostree support? The docs suggest it to be the dependency
Add a build tag of containers_image_ostree_stub and it will not be built in.

BTW This should happen automatically if you don;t have ostree-devel installed.

```
cat hack/ostree_tag.sh
#!/bin/bash                                                                                                                                                                                                        
if ! pkg-config ostree-1 2> /dev/null ; then
        echo containers_image_ostree_stub
fi
```

This line in the Makefile sets up the buildtags
```
BUILDTAGS ?= seccomp $(shell hack/btrfs_tag.sh) $(shell hack/libdm_tag.sh) $(shell hack/btrfs_installed_tag.sh) $(shell hack/ostree_tag.sh) $(shell hack/selinux_tag.sh)
```

So did this and complied and it worked for me:

```console
sudo dnf remove -y  ostree-devel 
```

Thanks a lot @rhatdan
